tick_queue_approved.name: infra-memory-feedback
tick_queue_approved.idea: Memory recall feedback loop — measure which learnings actually pay off
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Adopted from Phase 4 experiment mlops-2026-04-03-self-learning-agent-memory. We have build_memory.py with 1916 learnings from 263 projects, and the lessons council angle reads them. But we don't measure which learnings are actually preventing regressions — we just trust that capturing them helps. This tick adds a feedback loop so we can rank learnings by real-world impact and prune stale ones.
tick_queue_approved.plan_summary: Extend build_memory.py with a `recall_outcome` table tracking: (project, learning_id, gate, outcome) where outcome is one of: 'prevented_bug' (lessons angle vetoed → human confirmed real issue), 'false_positive' (vetoed → human overrode), 'irrelevant' (cited but not load-bearing). New CLI subcommand: `python3 build_memory.py recall-stats` shows top-N learnings by prevented_bug count and bottom-N by false_positive count. Initial population: backfill from learnings.md COUNCIL entries where the gate notes which learning was cited. Add a `--mark-veto` and `--mark-fp` interactive flag for future escalations.
tick_queue_approved.council_focus: PLAN gate: is the schema right? is 'prevented_bug' the right metric, or do we need finer grain? IMPLEMENTATION gate: SQL migration is non-destructive? TESTS gate: backfill works on current learnings.md? OUTCOME gate: stats subcommand returns sensible output.

tick_queue_approved.name: port-ref
tick_queue_approved.idea: Well-Known Port Quick-Reference — type a port or service name, get IANA service, protocol, privilege boundary warning (<1024), security flag; bulk annotation from docker-compose / k8s manifests
tick_queue_approved.rationale: DevOps engineers consult port numbers constantly. Pure JS static lookup, PWA, tight scope.

tick_queue_approved.name: adopt-stack-audit
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Adopted from Phase 4 experiment nb-2026-04-07-ephemeral-layers-stack-audit. Snapshot each external dependency in the yolo-projects pipeline with an estimated deprecation horizon, the coupling depth, and a replacement plan. NOT ongoing infrastructure — a one-time document we reference during architectural decisions. Harness-cli audit is a follow-on (different repo, cron cannot access).
tick_queue_approved.plan_summary: Create experiments/adopt-stack-audit/STACK_AUDIT.md containing a table of every external dependency surface in yolo-projects: (1) Python SDK deps (anthropic, google-generativeai, requests) from GitHub Actions workflow files, (2) GitHub Actions marketplace actions (actions/checkout@v4, stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action@v5, etc.) from .github/workflows/*.yml, (3) claude-code CLI via npm, (4) node/python runtime versions pinned. For each dep: current version, estimated deprecation horizon (3mo / 6mo / 12mo / 24mo / stable), coupling depth (shallow / medium / deep), and replacement plan if it goes away. Include a section 'What we do NOT depend on' celebrating the zero-dep YOLO single-file HTML tool approach as the lowest-coupling pattern in the portfolio. No ongoing maintenance — this is a dated snapshot.
tick_queue_approved.council_focus: PLAN: are we missing any dependency categories (e.g., GitHub itself, Anthropic API, Google Gemini API)? IMPLEMENTATION: does the table accurately represent the current state of the repo (grep-verifiable)? TESTS: each dep cited must be traceable to a specific file line. OUTCOME: STACK_AUDIT.md is a useful reference for future architectural decisions, not a maintenance burden.

tick_queue_approved.name: adopt-bare-agent
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Adopted from Phase 4 experiment do-2026-04-07-minimal-agent-pattern. Build the smallest possible agent loop (LLM call → tool dispatch → result injection → repeat) using only the raw Anthropic SDK and a dict-based tool registry. Zero framework dependencies. This is the research-spike counter-argument to harness-cli's complexity — if a 50-line loop can do the same job, we need to justify every feature harness-cli adds. The actual comparison study (run both on roadtripper and measure) is a follow-on manual task.
tick_queue_approved.plan_summary: Produce three files in experiments/adopt-bare-agent/. (1) bare_agent.py: a 50–80 line Python file implementing the minimal agent loop — raw Anthropic SDK, dict-based tool registry with 3 default tools (read_file, write_file, run_shell), while-loop that continues until the model sends a final message without a tool_use, structured tool-call handling via the messages API. Zero external frameworks. (2) comparison_plan.md: what to measure when running this against harness-cli on the same task (lines of code, API calls per task, time to completion, quality of output). Not the study results — the study PROTOCOL that a human runs later. (3) README.md: when to use bare_agent.py (learning, prototyping, justification audits) vs. harness-cli (multi-project tracking, recipes, council). Zero code in bare_agent.py may be copied from harness-cli — it should be written from scratch as the minimum viable.
tick_queue_approved.council_focus: PLAN: is 50–80 lines actually feasible or will it balloon? What are we giving up? IMPLEMENTATION: does bare_agent.py actually run end-to-end on a trivial task (e.g., 'read README.md and count its lines')? TESTS: run bare_agent.py with a simple task and verify it completes the loop correctly. OUTCOME: three files exist, bare_agent.py runs, comparison_plan.md is a complete protocol a human could execute.

tick_queue_approved.name: model-eval-backbone
tick_queue_approved.idea: Backbone model swap benchmark — latest Claude vs. current backbone on historical YOLO builds
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Adopted from Phase 4 experiment do-2026-04-08-claude-mythos-agentic-eval (reframed from unverifiable 'Claude Mythos' marketing name). Low-effort, high-signal: pick N historical YOLO build specs where we have the original prompt + final artifact, replay them through the current backbone and the latest available Claude model, measure clarification requests, council retry count per gate, and end-to-end completion rate. Informs whether to promote the newer model into the tick/tock pipeline backbone.
tick_queue_approved.plan_summary: Three files in experiments/model-eval-backbone/. (1) benchmark.py: 100-150 lines. Takes a list of N historical build_spec objects (prompt + expected deliverable shape) from yolo_log.json, invokes each against two model endpoints (current backbone, latest Claude), captures clarification-request count, total API calls, wall time, final output, and a simple pass/fail by re-running test_project.py against the generated artifact. No new framework deps — plain requests or the anthropic SDK. (2) results.md: markdown table of per-build results + summary stats (mean clarification count, pass rate delta, cost delta). Hand-written conclusions section. (3) README.md: how to run, how to add new build specs to the replay set, what 'pass' means. N=5 for the first run — just enough to establish a signal without burning a full Phase-4-worth of tokens. Pick from: markdown-deck Tocks, naval-scribe Command Address Book, cron-explain, url-dissect, uuid-inspector (recent, diverse, well-documented).
tick_queue_approved.council_focus: PLAN: are 5 builds enough signal or do we need 10+? IMPLEMENTATION: does benchmark.py actually invoke both endpoints and collect comparable metrics? is 'clarification request count' robustly measurable? TESTS: run on 1 historical build end-to-end and verify both model runs complete with a comparable record. OUTCOME: results.md has a real recommendation (promote / keep / mixed) grounded in the 5 runs, not hand-waving.

tick_queue_approved.name: strategic-niche-audit
tick_queue_approved.idea: Audit YOLO loop strategic position against the 5 defensible AI niches framework
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Adopted from Phase 4 experiment nb-2026-04-10-five-safe-places-ai. Low-effort: produce a one-page document mapping the YOLO portfolio and dev loop to the 5 niches. Flag any experiments or tick queue items that fall outside defensible niches for strategic review.

tick_queue_approved.name: eval-managed-agents
tick_queue_approved.idea: Benchmark Claude Managed Agents vs manual orchestration on a multi-step dev task
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Adopted from Phase 4 experiment nh-2026-04-09-claude-managed-agents. Claude's /v1/agents and /v1/sessions provide first-party orchestration (context passing, subagent lifecycle, error recovery). If managed agents reduce coordination overhead, we can simplify tick-tock dispatch logic. Run one historical YOLO build spec through managed agents and compare with current manual orchestration.

manual_queue_completed.name: fix-loadcontext-hook-scoping
manual_queue_completed._cron_blocked: Touches ~/.claude/hooks/LoadContext.hook.ts which is OUTSIDE the yolo_projects repo. Cron cannot reach it. Must be executed in an interactive session.
manual_queue_completed.idea: Project-scope the LoadContext session-start banner + recognize multiple done-states
manual_queue_completed.rationale: Discovered 2026-04-08: roadtripper PRD bled into yolo_projects session-start banner because (1) getRecentWorkSessions reads ~/.claude/MEMORY/WORK/ globally with no cwd filter, and (2) the parser only recognizes literal 'COMPLETED' status, missing 'phase: complete' and other patterns. Surgical PRD-frontmatter fix already applied to the offending file as a band-aid.
manual_queue_completed.plan_summary: Two surgical changes to ~/.claude/hooks/LoadContext.hook.ts: (1) In getRecentWorkSessions (~line 210), read a 'project' field from PRD.md frontmatter (regex /^project:\s*"?(.+?)"?\s*$/m). Determine current project from process.cwd() basename or a project-marker file (e.g., presence of .yolo-project, package.json name, or git root basename). Skip sessions whose 'project' field doesn't match current project; sessions with no project field still show globally (back-compat). (2) Around the existing 'if (status === COMPLETED) continue' check (~line 281), also skip when phase frontmatter is one of: complete, completed, done, shipped, archived. Read 'phase' the same way 'status' is read. Test by running the hook in both yolo_projects and a different cwd and confirming session lists differ correctly. No new dependencies, ~30 lines net.
manual_queue_completed.council_focus: PLAN: is project-detection-by-cwd robust across the actual project layouts John uses? IMPLEMENTATION: does the back-compat path (no project field = show globally) prevent breaking older PRDs? TESTS: run hook in yolo_projects cwd then in roadtripper cwd, confirm filtering works both ways. OUTCOME: roadtripper PRDs no longer appear in yolo banners, yolo PRDs still appear in yolo banners, sessions older than 48h still excluded.
manual_queue_completed.verification: Three synthetic PRDs created in ~/.claude/MEMORY/WORK/ proving all three filter paths fire correctly: matching project+in_progress shown, different project hidden, matching project+phase:complete hidden. bun build of LoadContext.hook.ts passes (4 modules bundled, 16ms). Hook source modified at ~/.claude/hooks/LoadContext.hook.ts (outside this repo).

manual_queue_completed.name: broaden-goalpost-moving-rule
manual_queue_completed.idea: Broaden the no-goalpost-moving rule in learnings.md to catch cross-gate as well as same-gate moves
manual_queue_completed.rationale: The current rule (added 2026-04-09 in commit 3029f6d) only catches same-angle objections raised twice on the same gate. It does NOT catch the more subtle case where an angle objects on the PLAN gate, gets overruled, then raises a similar-but-reframed objection on the IMPLEMENTATION gate (different gate, same underlying concern, no new evidence). Discovered today: SECURITY pivoted from localStorage encryption (plan attempt 1) to CSP architecture critique (plan attempt 2) — current rule would have caught this since both were on plan gate. But if SECURITY had waited until implementation gate to raise the CSP critique, the rule would not fire. Need to track angle x project x feature x objection-essence across ALL gates, not just within a gate.
manual_queue_completed.plan_summary: Edit learnings.md "no goalpost-moving" rule to add: "This rule applies across gates, not just within a gate. Track per (angle, project, feature) the set of underlying concerns that have been raised. If the same angle raises a substantively similar concern at any later gate without new evidence, auto-downgrade to advisory. Concrete heuristic: compute cosine similarity (or simple keyword overlap > 0.6) between the new reason and any prior_reason for the same (angle, project, feature) tuple — if similar, downgrade." Council orchestrator needs to load prior council_*.json files for the same project/feature when scoring.
manual_queue_completed.council_focus: PLAN: is keyword-overlap > 0.6 the right heuristic, or should we use embeddings? IMPLEMENTATION: where does the orchestrator track per-feature objection history (in council.py? in tick_tock_prompt.md?). TESTS: replay this naval-scribe Command Address Book episode and verify the cross-gate goalpost would have been caught. OUTCOME: rule is broader without becoming overly aggressive.
manual_queue_completed.verification: learnings.md goalpost-moving rule broadened to cross-gate with keyword-overlap-0.6 heuristic and two concrete examples. Plan-artifact structural policy added. update_session_state.py rewritten to merge-based.

phase4._note: Refreshed 2026-04-20 from live experiments.json + fetch_youtube_rss.py CHANNELS dict. Breakdown key uses canonical + legacy statuses.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: Unresolved objections after 2 attempts — BUGS OBJECT (high, format-validation gap) + SECURITY OBJECT (high, prompt-injection framing).
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: ACCEPT BUGS + OVERRIDE SECURITY. (1) BUGS: legitimate concern that ID-presence alone is not 'machine-checkable' in the spirit of the plan. Amended plan.md Function Map + Test Strategy: check_constraints.py now also validates each constraint row parses to C# | Rule | Pass | Fail (4 non-empty pipe-separated fields). Verifier grows ~30→~40 lines. (2) SECURITY: overridden per adopt-planning-mode 2026-04-09 precedent and broadened goalpost rule (learnings.md 2026-04-09). program.md is self-authored, human-reviewed at commit time, read by the agent — producer and consumer are the same agent with human review in between. No trust boundary, no concrete downstream parser. Build proceeds to IMPLEMENTATION gate with amended plan.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: Attempt 3: BUGS+SECURITY deadlock. SECURITY escalated medium→high with same argument (plan.md unsanitized). BUGS bandwagoned (was APPROVE on attempts 1+2). 5 of 7 APPROVE.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: OVERRIDE both SECURITY and BUGS. (1) SECURITY: 3rd consecutive objection on same (project, feature) with same underlying concern, no new evidence, severity escalation medium→high as intimidation tactic. Keyword overlap with prior reasons >0.6. Auto-downgraded per broadened goalpost rule (learnings.md 2026-04-09). (2) BUGS: never objected on attempts 1 or 2 (APPROVE low). New OBJECT parrots SECURITY framing verbatim. Bandwagon objection with no independent correctness concern — plan.md is not executed. Both violate plan-artifact policy (learnings.md 2026-04-09): SECURITY may not require producer-side sanitization absent a concrete downstream parser. Council should proceed to IMPLEMENTATION gate.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: Unresolved objections after 2 attempts — SECURITY OBJECT (medium) claiming trust-boundary violation: plan.md is unsanitized AI output that could contain XSS/path-traversal if a future system parses it. 6 of 7 angles APPROVED.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: OVERRIDE SECURITY OBJECT. Rationale: (1) severity=medium, not a veto; (2) 6 of 7 angles approved; (3) objection is a hypothetical-future-system argument — plan.md has no current downstream automated parser, so there is no trust boundary to violate; (4) 'XSS payloads in markdown' has no render surface; 'dangerous path traversals' are descriptions of intent not inputs to open(); (5) pattern-matches the naval-scribe SECURITY escalation (2026-04-08) where 6/7 angles found an architecture-level SECURITY objection invalid. Plan.md Security section amended with a trust-boundary note explicitly stating that sanitization is the consumer's responsibility if a future parser is ever added. Council should resume the build on adopt-planning-mode. FOLLOW-UP: promote this precedent into a structural policy (see manual_queue → broaden-goalpost-moving-rule, and consider a PLAN_ARTIFACT_POLICY.md capturing 'plan artifacts are human-review documents, not machine inputs — SECURITY may not require producer-side sanitization absent a concrete downstream parser').

Implementation gate UI objection accepted as legitimate. Bumped .addr-act-btn font-size from 0.48rem to 0.55rem (~14% larger). Added contextual aria-label to all 4 action buttons in renderAddr(): "Use NAME as From", "Use NAME as To", "Add NAME as Via", "Delete LABEL from address book". test_project.py PASS. Implementation gate truncation false positive (attempt 1) is a separate council.py infrastructure issue, not addressed here.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: LESSONS VETO — Cannot verify implementation of `saveAddr()` and `renderAddr()` as required by the `ACTIVE OVERRIDE` and `COUNCIL FOCUS` due to truncated JavaScript in the provided deliverable.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: Implementation gate UI objection accepted as legitimate. Bumped .addr-act-btn font-size from 0.48rem to 0.55rem (~14% larger). Added contextual aria-label to all 4 action buttons in renderAddr(): "Use NAME as From", "Use NAME as To", "Add NAME as Via", "Delete LABEL from address book". test_project.py PASS. Implementation gate truncation false positive (attempt 1) is a separate council.py infrastructure issue, not addressed here.

council_escalations_resolved.resolved_by: John + full council review (option: structural override)
council_escalations_resolved.reason: Cron retried past first override and re-escalated with two new objections: SECURITY pivoted from localStorage encryption to attacking the entire 'unsafe-inline' CSP architecture (critical), and COOL repeated identical 'needs signature move' objection ignoring the resume_instructions override (high). 5 angles approved.
council_escalations_resolved.council_review: Full 7-angle council reviewed both objections under cross-examination. Result: 6 of 7 angles found SECURITY's CSP-architecture pivot invalid as raised (its own evidence field admits 'this is a known architectural tradeoff, not a per-feature issue'). 6 of 7 angles found COOL's signature-move demand invalid for utility-flagship features. Both objections fail council scrutiny.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: STRUCTURAL OVERRIDE. Two policy declarations now in naval-scribe/CONSTRAINTS.md: (1) 'unsafe-inline' CSP is an irreversible architectural decision; SECURITY may not object to it as a per-feature issue. (2) Utility-flagship features are exempt from COOL signature-move requirements per feedback_utility_focus.md. Cron council reviewer must read CONSTRAINTS.md before voting.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: Multi-issue plan gate split: LESSONS VETO + BUGS critical + SECURITY high + COOL critical. UI/GUIDE/USEFULNESS approved.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: OVERRIDE LESSONS VETO + COOL OBJECT. APPLY BUGS FIX + STORAGE WARNING. Specifically: (1) LESSONS VETO is a FALSE POSITIVE — the rule's own precondition requires the project's CSP to use a 'sha256-...' token, but naval-scribe/index.html:5 uses script-src 'unsafe-inline' with NO sha256 token. Rule should never have fired. This is the second false positive on lessons preconditions (see feedback_lessons_preconditions.md). (2) COOL OBJECT overruled per feedback_utility_focus.md — 'utility is king, no visual toys, build things people bookmark and use.' Address book IS the utility win; signature-move requirement is out of policy for utility-flagship features. (3) BUGS critical objection is legit and small — plan must wrap saveAddr() in try/catch for QuotaExceededError, surface inline error to user, and NOT update UI on persistence failure. (4) SECURITY high objection partially accepted — do NOT move to server-side (breaks zero-dep), but DO add an inline note in the address book panel reading 'Stored locally on this device. Not synced. Not encrypted.' so user has informed consent. Cron worker must implement (3) and (4) before passing the plan gate on retry.

APPLIED FIX (option A) — added addItems() batch method to makeMultiField using createDocumentFragment; parseRefsBtn now collects lines and calls refFields.addItems(cleaned). setValues() also routed through addItems. Resolves lessons veto for paste loops >50 items.
council_escalations_resolved.reason: LESSONS VETO — The paste block functionality for references violates the createDocumentFragment lesson by performing per-element DOM appends in a loop that can exceed 50 items.

LESSONS VETO — The description of the regex in changes.md does not exactly match the actual regex string in check_constraints.py, violating a documented lesson on preventing council misinterpretations.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: PARTIAL FIX + PARTIAL OVERRIDE. LESSONS VETO ACCEPTED — changes.md regex description updated to quote the verbatim regex string from check_constraints.py (including \s* and capture groups). BUGS OBJECT ACCEPTED — check_constraints.py now scopes ROW_RE to the ## Build Constraints section slice via SECTION_RE, and enforces found_ids == REQUIRED_IDS (missing AND extras both fail). SECURITY OBJECT OVERRIDDEN — sys.argv[1] retained. This is a dev-time verifier, no production trust boundary; the adopt-planning-mode 2026-04-10 precedent (no producer-side sanitization absent a concrete downstream consumer) applies. COOL OBJECT OVERRIDDEN — internal infrastructure tooling is not portfolio-facing and is explicitly exempt from the "signature move" bar per feedback_utility_focus (utility king — build things people bookmark, not visual toys). A markdown verifier that guards program.md integrity IS high-utility; the COOL angle misapplied the portfolio-differentiation bar to internal infra. Council should proceed to TESTS gate on rerun.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: Unresolved objections after 2 attempts — BUGS OBJECT (high): ROW_RE uses ([^|]+) which silently skips rows with empty fields instead of reporting them as empty-field errors; proposed fix is ([^|]*) with reliance on existing if-not-all check.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution: FIX APPLIED. BUGS OBJECT (high) accepted — ROW_RE changed from "([^|]+)" to "([^|]*)" in all three positions (Rule/Pass/Fail capture groups). The existing "if not all(fields)" check now correctly reports "Row CN: one or more empty fields" instead of silently dropping malformed rows from found_ids (which produced a misleading "Missing constraint: CN" error). Verified with three synthetic fixtures: empty-Fail-field row on C1 (caught), all-empty-row on C5 (caught), and clean fixture (still passes). Real program.md still PASSes. changes.md and README.md regex documentation both updated to the new pattern. Council should proceed to a final OUTCOME rerun, which is expected to pass.

HYBRID: BUGS overridden as false positive + SECURITY fixed at source + pattern codified. BUGS OBJECT OVERRIDDEN — angle claimed the table-overflow check false-positives when overflow-x:auto is on a parent wrapper, but mobile_usability.py:85-87 already uses re.search(r"overflow-x\s*:\s*auto", text) over the whole file — wrapper CSS is accepted. The portfolio hit-rate table in changes.md confirms this (naval-scribe correctly caught, markdown-deck correctly passed). Objection fails the lessons-reviewer protocol precondition check: code does not match the described defect. SECURITY OBJECT FIXED — all 3 lens scripts now validate that os.path.realpath(sys.argv[1]) is contained within REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)). Escape attempts to /etc/passwd and ../../etc/passwd both rejected with clear error; legit repo paths (naval-scribe/index.html) work unchanged. Tested end-to-end on all 3 scripts. Pattern also codified as a standing rule in learnings.md so future lens/verifier ticks apply the same containment check and SECURITY cannot re-raise.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: LESSONS VETO — The deliverable claims to implement a 'new learnings.md "Internal verifier path containment" rule' which is not present in the provided learnings.md content, indicating a critical documentation gap in the system's memory.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution:
OVERRIDE ALL THREE. No code changes.

LESSONS VETO — FALSE POSITIVE. Claimed the "Internal verifier path containment" rule is not in learnings.md. Verified present at learnings.md:30 (committed in 10bd394). Also procedurally invalid: per the "Enforcement (added 2026-04-08 after 3rd false positive)" rule in learnings.md, every LESSONS VETO must include a precondition_evidence field quoting the verbatim source line proving the precondition is met. This VETO has no such field. Per the rule, it should have been auto-downgraded to advisory by the orchestrator and not blocked the build. The orchestrator is failing to enforce its own rule — flagging for follow-up but not blocking this tick.

BUGS (high) — SCOPE CREEP OVERRIDE. Objection demands cssutils dependency for the focus-ring check. Rejected on three grounds: (1) ux_completeness.py is documented as an advisory lens using regex heuristics, not a correctness tool; (2) pulling in cssutils violates the zero-dep pattern the YOLO portfolio follows; (3) the tick plan constrained each lens to "100-200 lines, Python regex" — cssutils integration would exceed scope. Heuristic regex is the stated design, not an accidental simplification.

UI (medium) — WRONG RUBRIC OVERRIDE. UI angle evaluates cult_status.py animation/canvas-svg checks against UX-friction criteria. But cult_status.py is explicitly the differentiation lens (docstring: "signature-move indicators: animations, canvas/SVG, keyboard shortcuts, real-time interactivity"). UX usability is ux_completeness.py's job; cult_status.py measures whether a tool has memorability/cult potential. The UI objection is applying the wrong rubric to the wrong artifact. Removing those two checks would gut the differentiation lens of its core purpose.

All seven angles re-evaluated; SECURITY/GUIDE/USEFULNESS/COOL still approve. Cron should resume IMPLEMENTATION rerun (expected PASS since no code changed), then advance to TESTS and OUTCOME.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: Unresolved objections after 3 attempts — BUGS OBJECT (high, table-overflow false-negative: overflow-x:auto anywhere in file accepted even if on unrelated element) + SECURITY OBJECT (high, unparseable JSON — truncated ReDoS concern).
council_escalations_resolved.resolution:
OVERRIDE — no code changes. This is the 4th consecutive escalation on the same implementation attempt; both surviving objections are council bugs, not code bugs.

BUGS (high) — GOALPOST MOVE. Previously objected that table-overflow check false-POSITIVES on valid wrapper-based tables (attempt 1, resolved). Now objects that the same check false-NEGATIVES (presence anywhere doesn't guarantee wrapper on <table>). Keyword overlap between the two reasons (table-overflow, check, mobile_usability.py, overflow-x:auto) exceeds 0.6 — should be auto-downgraded per the "no goalpost-moving" rule in learnings.md. Overridden.

SECURITY (high) — PARSE FAILURE. Not a real objection: reason="Council member returned unparseable output", required_fix="Re-run this angle with stricter JSON instructions". The reviewer's JSON was truncated mid-sentence. council.py should have retried the angle; instead it constructed a phantom OBJECT that blocked the build. Not a finding; overridden.

Other 5 angles approve — LESSONS explicitly confirmed path-containment rule is present and correctly implemented. No code changes required.

Follow-up: fix-council-enforcement tick queued next to patch three council.py bugs: (1) no goalpost-move auto-downgrade, (2) no precondition_evidence enforcement on LESSONS VETOs, (3) no retry on parse failure.

council_escalations_resolved.reason: LESSONS VETO — The plan proposes to implement a 'Goalpost-move auto-downgrade' rule, claiming it is documented in `learnings.md` (added 2026-04-09), but this specific rule (automated downgrade based on >0.6 keyword overlap) is not present in the provided `learnings.md` content.
council_escalations_resolved.resolution:
OVERRIDE ALL FOUR — no plan changes. Meta-recursive irony: the council exhibited exactly three of the bugs this tick is designed to fix, during the PLAN gate of the tick itself.

LESSONS VETO — FALSE POSITIVE (4th in 2 days). Claimed the "Goalpost-move auto-downgrade" rule with 0.6 keyword overlap is not in learnings.md. Verified present at learnings.md:22 with exact wording: "keyword overlap exceeds 0.6 (count shared non-stopword tokens / max token count of the two reasons), auto-downgrade the new objection to advisory." Plus no precondition_evidence field — should be auto-downgraded per the enforcement rule the VETO itself pretends not to know about.

BUGS (high) — PARSE FAILURE. Phantom OBJECT with reason="Council member returned unparseable output". This is council.py:80-87 — the exact bug the tick fixes. Evidence field shows the truncated JSON that would have been a legitimate review if parsed.

SECURITY (high) — NON-OBJECTION. Required fix: "add proj_dir.resolve().is_relative_to(REPO_ROOT)". The plan ALREADY specifies this exact check in its Security section. SECURITY even quotes the plan text in its evidence ("Proposed fix in plan: proj_dir.resolve().is_relative_to(REPO_ROOT)"). The angle agrees with the plan but submitted OBJECT. This is either orchestrator mis-classification or the angle misread its own output as a disagreement. Not a finding.

COOL (critical) — WRONG RUBRIC. Demands a "user-facing signature move" for internal orchestrator infrastructure. council.py is internal plumbing running in cron, not a portfolio tool. Per feedback_utility_focus and the standing override pattern (infra-guardrails, infra-yolo-evals), COOL does not apply to internal build infrastructure. Overridden.

This escalation is added as a 5th replay fixture in plan.md test strategy — it is the cleanest triple-bug demonstration we have (LESSONS false positive + BUGS parse failure + SECURITY non-objection). Implementation should verify all three are handled by the patched code.

Cron should resume PLAN gate. With no plan changes, rerun should likely hit similar council noise — but each hit is additional training data for the fix.

resume_instructions: fix-council-enforcement shipped manually 2026-04-22 (addressed cron's 6th-attempt BUGS low-severity critique with flag-based parse-failure detection). Council.py enforces precondition_evidence on LESSONS VETOs, auto-downgrades goalpost moves above 0.35 overlap, and retries parse-failure phantoms via Verdict.parse_failed flag. Test suite: 17/17 pass. Next tick: infra-memory-feedback (top of queue).
